content.full_text:
Fnrn Polrrrcnr- PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 15,2010
Robin Stieler
Clerk of the Board of Supervisors
333 W. Santa Ana Blvd. Suite 465
Santa Ana, CA 92701
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. ,{-10-022
Dear Ms. Stieler:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the reporting provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTTONS
1. May members of the board of directors of a local agency who are also Orange
County Supervisors or City Council members file their Form 700 electronically under
Orange County's pilot program authorized,by Section 87500.1?
2. May public officials who manage public investments for a local agency, board
or commission as specified under Section 87200, file their Form 700 electronically under
Orange County's pilot program per Section 87500.1?
3. May a local agency, board or commission designate in its conflict of interest
code the Board of Supervisors as the filing officer for public officials who manage public
investments as specified under Section 87200?
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California
Ctlde of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califurnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-10-022
Page No.2
CONCLUSION
l. Yes, but only if the supervisors or city council members who are sitting on a
board or local agency file in a capacity other than as an 87200 filer.2 However, please
note that the electronic filings of the officials' statements of economic interests are in
addition to the separate statement required for their position as 87200 filers. Members of
county boards of supervisors or city council members are not required to file statements
of economic interests for any other agency which has the same jurisdiction as the board
of supervisors (the county), or a smaller jurisdiction wholly contained within the county.
See discussion below.
2-3. No. Public officials who manage public investments for a local agency,
board or commission are 87200 filers and may not file their Form 700 electronically
under Orange County's pilot program authorized,by Section 87500.1. Officials whose
position is enumerated in Section 87200 may not participate in the pilot program and may
not be designated in the county's conflict of interest code. See discussion below.
FACTS
You are the Board Services Manager of the Orange County Clerk to the Board of
Supervisors. You are writing on behalf of Darlene Boom, who is the Orange County
Clerk of the Board of Supervisors.
You seek guidance regarding the filing of Statements of Economic Interests
("Form 700") by local county officials and designated employees who serve on agencies,
boards and commissions in the county.
Specifically, you wish to know whether members of the board of directors of a
local agency who are also Orange County Supervisors or City Council members may file
their Form 700 electronically under Orange County's pilot program authorizedby
Section 87500.1. You also wish to know if public officials who manage public
investments for a local agency, board or commission as specified under Section 87200,
may file their Form 700 electronically under Orange County's pilot program, and if the
county may designate in its conflict-of-interest code the Board of Supervisors as the
filing officer for these public officials.
AI\ALYSIS
Under the Act, state and local "public officials" file periodic statements of
economic interests (Form 700) for the purpose of disclosing pertinent economic interests.
(See Sections 87200 et seq.; 87300 et seq.; Section 82048 [defining "public official"].)
2 These are public otficials whose positions are identified in Section 8'7200.


File No. A-lO-022
Page No. 3
These individuals are required to file the Form 700 pursuant to either Section 87200 or
Section 87300.
Section 87500.1 and Officials Subject to Section g7200
Section 87500.1, which became effective January l,2OOg, and is set to expire
March 12,2012, authorized the counties of Los Angeles, Merced, Orange and Stanislaus
to participate in a pilot program allowing the electronic filing of Form 7b0s.
Counties participating in the pilot program may allow certain officials to file
electronically their Forms 700 and must notify the filer that the filing was received,
among other things.
The program explicitly prohibits certain public officials from participating.
Specifically, a public official subject to Section 87200 "shall not participate in the pilot
program." (Section 87500. 1(b).)
Section 87200lists those officials with the broadest decision-making authority,
including certain high-ranking state and local officials like the county boarjof
supervisors, members of city councils, and public officials who manage public
investments, and candidates for any of these offices at any election ur-d imposes on them
the broadest disclosure requirements under the Act.l
However, please note that members of county boards of supervisors or city
council members ate not required to file statements of economic interests for any other
agency that has the same jurisdiction as the board of supervisors (the county), or a
smaller jurisdiction wholly contained within the county. (Lefalt Advice I-eiier, No. A-99-
049; Regulation 18730, enclosed.)
For instance, if the jurisdiction of a local board or agency is the same as, or
wholly included within the jurisdiction of the Orange Couniy Board of Supervisors, a
member of the Board of Supervisors who also holds a position with the og"n"y is not
required under the Act to file any statements of economic interests with the agency. The
official is deemed to be in compliance with the requirements of the Act by fili"ng
statements of economic interests as a member of the Board of Supervis ort. e"i"tt Advice
Letter, supra.)
Therefore, members of the board of supervisors or city council may participate in
Orange County's pilot program, but the electronic filings of their statements of economic
interests are in addition to the separate statement required for their positions as g72OO
filers.
I For officials not listed in Section 872oo,the Act requires state and local government agencies to
determine their disclosure requirements through the adoption of a conflict-of-intereit code. (Section
87300.)


File No. A-lO-022
Page No.4
Confltct-of-lnterest Codes: Publtc Officials Who Manage Public Investments
"Other public officials who manage public investments" must file financial
disclosure forms pursuant to Section 87200. Individuals who file statements pursuant to
Section 87200 do so because their position is specifically enumerated in that iection,
rather than because they are designated in an agency's conflict-of-interest code.
Section 87200 filers have a uniform disclosure level throughout the state that does
not vary with each agency's unique conflict-of-interest code. We have advised that no
individual whose position is enumerated in Section 87200 may be designated in an
agency's conflict-of-interest code. This includes "members of local government
agencies, boards or commissions who manage public investments on behalf of their
agency" and who file their statements of economic interests with the county as their filing
officer pursuant to Regulation 18753. (Btondo Advice Letter, A-95-256, enclosed;
Sections 82019(bXl), 87302(b).)
As previously discussed, officials who file pursuant to Section87200 "shall not
participate in the pilot program." (Section 87500.1(b).) Accordingly, by virtue of their
status as 87200 filers, public officials who manage public investments for a local agency,
board or commission may not be included in the county's conflict-of-interest code, and-
they may not file their Form 700 electronically under Orange County's pilot program
authorized by Section 87500.1.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
lt
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ERjgl
Enclosures

code the Board of Supervisors as the filing officer for public officials who manage public
investments as specified under Section 87200?

CONCLUSION: l. Yes, but only if the supervisors or city council members who are sitting on a
